title: Reinforcement Learning
subtitle: RL agents built from scratch - explore the code and play each one live

projects.title: Q-Learning Tic Tac Toe
projects.summary: tabular Q-learning -> dict[(state, action)] -> Bellman update
projects.description: A tabular Q-learner that picks moves via epsilon-greedy over a dict mapping (state, action) to estimated value. Trained against a uniformly-random opponent for 10,000 episodes. States are frozen 3x3 board snapshots; actions are (row, col) coordinates.
bullets: alpha=0.1 learning rate - how aggressively new estimates override old
gamma=0.9 discount - values future reward 0.9x per step
epsilon=0.1 exploration - 1 in 10 moves is random, keeping coverage alive
1134 (state, action) pairs in the Q-table - full coverage of reachable positions

projects.title: Q-Learning Tic Tac Toe (Self-Play)
projects.summary: tabular Q-learning -> two agents play each other -> Bellman update
projects.description: Two tabular Q-learners trained against each other via self-play. Both use epsilon-greedy with linear decay from 0.2 to 0.05, paired with optimistic Q-init at 1.0 so unseen moves get tried before epsilon-exploration kicks in. After each move, the opponent's prior (state, action) is updated with the resulting next-board so both sides learn from every non-terminal step.
bullets: alpha=1.0 learning rate - deterministic environment, every update is believed outright
gamma=1.0 discount - finite 9-move horizon, terminal reward propagates undiminished
epsilon decays 0.2 -> 0.05 over 80% of training, with optimistic Q-init=1.0 driving early exploration
self-play - two Q-tables, each learning against the other (30k episodes)

projects.title: Deep Q-Network — Atari Breakout
projects.summary: CNN Q-approximator -> replay buffer + target network -> Huber + Adam
projects.description: A convolutional DQN trained from raw Atari pixels. The Q-table is replaced by a 3-conv / 2-fc network that maps a (4, 84, 84) stack of grayscale frames to Q-values over the 4 Breakout actions. A 100k-capacity replay buffer decorrelates samples and a frozen target network (synced every 1000 steps) stabilises the bootstrap target. Trained 3000 episodes on CPU.
projects.headline_stat: 30 eval games | avg 15.6 | best 24 | worst 9 | σ 4.2
bullets: DQN CNN - Conv(8/4) -> Conv(4/2) -> Conv(3/1) -> FC(512) -> FC(4) over (4,84,84) stacked frames
replay buffer (100k) + target network synced every 1000 steps - the two tricks that make neural Q-learning converge
gamma=0.99, Adam lr=1e-4, batch 32, 10k warmup, epsilon decays 1.0 -> 0.1 over 100k env steps
Huber loss over MSE - caps gradient magnitude on large TD errors so outlier rewards don't blow up training

projects.title: Dueling Double DQN — Atari Breakout
projects.summary: Dueling CNN + Double DQN + Prioritized Replay + 4-step returns -> 27.9 avg over 50 games
projects.description: A Dueling Double DQN trained from raw Atari pixels with Prioritized Experience Replay and n-step returns. The shared CNN trunk splits into a Value head V(s) and Advantage head A(s,a), combined as Q = V + (A - mean A). Double DQN decouples action selection from evaluation to cut overestimation bias. A SumTree-backed PER buffer samples high-TD-error transitions more often, and 4-step returns propagate reward further back in time. Trained 70,001 episodes on Apple MPS.
projects.headline_stat: 50 eval games | avg 27.9 | best 80 | worst 11 | σ 12.1
bullets: Dueling CNN - shared Conv(8/4) -> Conv(4/2) -> Conv(3/1) trunk, split into Value head FC(512)->1 and Advantage head FC(512)->4
Double DQN - online net selects next action, target net evaluates it, removing max-bias overestimation
Prioritized Experience Replay - SumTree samples by |TD error|^0.6, IS weights (β: 0.4->1.0) correct the bias
4-step returns + gamma=0.99, Adam lr=1e-4, batch 32, 1M replay buffer, epsilon decays 1.0 -> 0.01 over 2M steps
